docs.title: Phinder
docs.text: Phinder is a Java application that uses the Phileas library to identify PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in text across a wide variety of file formats.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Multi-format Support: Process Plain Text, PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, CSV, RTF, Emails, Log files, and Images.
  • Recursive Processing: Scan entire directories and their subdirectories.
  • Customizable Policies: Use Phileas policies to define what PII to look for.
  • Magnitude & Density Scoring: Calculate scores based on the types, counts, and concentration of PII found.
  • Reporting: Always generates HTML and JSON reports.
  • High Performance: Optimized for handling large files and millions of documents.

docs.title: How it Works
docs.text: Phinder extracts text from various file formats and uses Phileas's PlainTextFilterService to identify PII spans. It also checks file names for PII. Results are aggregated and can be exported as reports.

docs.title: CLI Options
docs.text:
Phinder is a command-line application. Below is a list of all available options.

Option Long Option Description -i --input Required. The input file(s) or directories to scan. Repeat for multiple. -R --recursive Recursively traverse subdirectories if directories are provided. -p --policy Path to a custom Phileas policy (JSON file). -w --weights Path to custom PII weights (JSON file). -l --log Enable the scan log using a MongoDB database. -s --skip-unchanged Skip scanning files that haven't changed since the last scan log. --clean Truncate the scan log database. --mongodb The MongoDB URI for the scan log and report history. --csv-delimiter Custom CSV delimiter character. (Default: ,) --csv-quote Custom CSV quote character. (Default: ") -h --help Show the help message and exit. -V --version Show the application version and exit.

docs.title: Scan multiple files
docs.text: java -jar phinder.jar -i file1.txt -i file2.docx

docs.title: Scan a directory recursively with custom weights
docs.text: java -jar phinder.jar -i my_docs/ -R -w weights.json

docs.title: What is Phinder?
docs.text: Phinder is a command-line application that identifies Personally Identifiable Information (PII) in various file formats, such as PDF, Word, Excel, and more. It uses the Phileas library for PII detection.

docs.title: Which file formats are supported?
docs.text: Phinder supports a wide range of formats including: - Plain Text (.txt, .log) - Adobe PDF (.pdf) - Microsoft Word (.doc, .docx) - Microsoft Excel (.xls, .xlsx) - Microsoft PowerPoint (.ppt, .pptx) - Comma-Separated Values (.csv) - Rich Text Format (.rtf) - Email Messages (.eml, .msg) - Images (.png, .jpg, .tiff) via OCR

docs.title: How do I define what PII to look for?
docs.text: You can provide a Phileas policy file in JSON format using the -p or --policy option. This policy defines the types of PII (e.g., names, SSNs, credit card numbers) to detect.

docs.title: Can I scan entire directories?
docs.text: Yes, Phinder can scan a single file or an entire directory. Use the -R or --recursive flag to scan subdirectories as well.

docs.title: How does Phinder handle large files?
docs.text: Phinder is designed for high performance. Large log files are processed line-by-line to minimize memory usage, and other formats are handled efficiently using specialized processors.

docs.title: What are Magnitude and Density scores?
docs.text:
• Magnitude Score: A weighted score representing the total amount of PII found in a document. You can customize the weights for different PII types.
  • Density Score: The ratio of PII findings to the total amount of text in the document.

docs.title: How can I speed up subsequent scans?
docs.text: You can use the --log and --skip-unchanged options. Phinder will maintain a log (in a MongoDB database) of the files it has already scanned and their hashes. On subsequent runs, it will skip files that haven't changed. You must provide the MongoDB connection string via the PHINDER_MONGODB_URI environment variable.

docs.title: In what formats are the reports generated?
docs.text: Phinder always generates both HTML (.html) and JSON (.json) reports.

docs.title: How is OCR handled for images?
docs.text: Phinder uses Tesseract for OCR. You may need to set the TESSDATA_PREFIX environment variable to the directory containing your Tesseract language data files (e.g., eng.traineddata).

docs.title: Does Phinder require an internet connection?
docs.text: No, Phinder performs all PII detection locally using the Phileas library and does not send your data to any external services.

docs.title: Does Phinder redact the PII?
docs.text: No, Phinder is designed for identification and reporting of PII. If you need to redact or anonymize PII, consider using Philter.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Java 21 or higher
  • Maven 3.x
  • Tesseract OCR (optional, required for processing images)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Clone the repository and build the project using Maven:

mvn clean install

This will create a runnable shaded JAR in the target/ directory.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
To scan a single text file:

java -jar target/phinder-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar -i input.txt

To scan a directory:

java -jar target/phinder-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar -i /path/to/my/files/

docs.title: Reports
docs.text: Phinder always generates an HTML and JSON report (report.html and report.json).

docs.title: Report Formats
docs.text: Format File Description HTML report.html A modern, visually attractive HTML report. JSON report.json A machine-readable JSON file.

docs.title: Generating a Report
docs.text: Reports are always generated at the end of a scan.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: java -jar phinder.jar -i input.txt

docs.title: Report Content
docs.text: Every report includes: 1. Report ID: A unique UUID for the scan. 2. Timestamp: The date and time the scan was completed. 3. Aggregate Magnitude Score: Total magnitude across all files. 4. Aggregate Density Score: Magnitude score divided by total word count across all files. 5. Aggregate PII Counts: Total number of occurrences for each PII type. 6. Files Skipped: The number of files skipped because they hadn't changed since the last scan. 7. PII Weights: The weights used for each PII type (if custom weights were provided). 8. Best Candidates for Redaction Testing: A table of the top 20 files with the most PII variety and highest scores (HTML format only). 9. Per-file Details: For each file, the Magnitude and Density Scores, and the counts of each PII type found.

docs.title: Report History
docs.text:
If the --mongodb option is used, the report will also be stored in the specified MongoDB database.

java -jar target/phinder-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT.jar -i src/test/resources/input.txt --mongodb "mongodb://localhost:27017/phinder"

docs.title: Supported File Types
docs.text:
Phinder identifies PII in both the file contents and the file name.

Category Extensions Notes Plain Text .txt, .md Markdown is supported. Documents .pdf, .docx, .doc, .rtf Spreadsheets .xlsx, .xls, .csv CSV delimiter and quoting can be customized. Presentations .pptx, .ppt Extracts from slides, notes, and comments. Email .eml, .msg Extracts subject, sender, and body. Logs .log Processed line-by-line for memory efficiency. Images .png, .jpg, .jpeg Requires Tesseract OCR installed.

Note

Processing images requires tesseract-ocr to be installed on your system.

docs.title: Processing Files
docs.text:
You can specify one or more files using the -i or --input option:

java -jar phinder.jar -i file1.txt -i file2.pdf

docs.title: Processing Directories
docs.text:
To process all supported files in a directory:

java -jar phinder.jar -i /path/to/directory

docs.title: Recursive Search
docs.text:
Use the -R or --recursive flag to traverse subdirectories:

java -jar phinder.jar -i /path/to/directory -R

docs.title: CSV Customization
docs.text:
When processing CSV files, you can specify the delimiter and quote character:

java -jar phinder.jar -i data.csv --csv-delimiter=";" --csv-quote="'"

docs.title: Large File Support
docs.text: Phinder is designed to handle very large files efficiently. Specifically, .log files are processed line-by-line to minimize heap usage.

docs.title: Scan Logging and Skipping
docs.text: To speed up subsequent scans, Phinder can maintain a log of scanned files and their xxHash64 hashes using a MongoDB database.

docs.title: MongoDB Configuration
docs.text:
To use the scan log, you must provide a MongoDB connection string via the PHINDER_MONGODB_URI environment variable:

export PHINDER_MONGODB_URI="mongodb://localhost:27017"

docs.title: Generating a Scan Log
docs.text:
By default, using --log or --skip-unchanged will enable the scan log using the MongoDB database specified in PHINDER_MONGODB_URI.

java -jar phinder.jar -i /path/to/data --log

docs.title: Skipping Unchanged Files
docs.text:
On the next run, use --skip-unchanged to skip any file that hasn't changed (based on its xxHash64 hash):

java -jar phinder.jar -i /path/to/data --skip-unchanged

Phinder will compare the file names and hash values in the MongoDB database to determine if a file can be skipped. The count of skipped files will be included in the reports.

When using the scan log, Phinder also stores a timestamped copy of the scan results in a reports collection in the MongoDB database. This allows for historical tracking of scan results.

docs.title: Cleaning the Scan Log
docs.text:
To clear the recorded hashes and paths from the scan log database, use the --clean option:

java -jar phinder.jar --clean

This will delete the entries in the scan log collections, forcing all files to be re-scanned on the next run with --skip-unchanged.

docs.title: Phileas Policy
docs.text: Phinder uses the Phileas library to perform PII detection. You can provide a custom Phileas policy in JSON format using the -p or --policy option.

docs.title: Example Policy
docs.text:
A policy defines which identifiers (PII types) to enable.

{
  "identifiers": {
    "emailAddress": {
      "emailAddressFilterStrategies": [
        {
          "strategy": "REDACT",
          "redaction": "[[EMAIL]]"
        }
      ]
    },
    "ssn": {
      "ssnFilterStrategies": [
        {
          "strategy": "REDACT",
          "redaction": "[[SSN]]"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Applying the Policy
docs.text:
java -jar phinder.jar -i input.txt -p my-policy.json

If no policy is provided, Phinder uses a default policy that looks for email addresses.

docs.title: Magnitude Score Weights
docs.text: Phinder calculates a Magnitude Score for each file and an aggregate score for the entire scan. By default, every PII occurrence has a weight of 1.0.

docs.title: Custom Weights
docs.text: You can specify custom weights for different PII types using a weights.json file. This allows you to prioritize certain types of PII over others (e.g., a SSN might have a higher magnitude than an email address).

docs.title: weights.json Format
docs.text:
The file should be a JSON object mapping PII types (as identified by Phileas) to numeric weights.

{
  "email-address": 2.0,
  "ssn": 10.0,
  "phone-number": 5.0
}

docs.title: Using Weights
docs.text:
Specify the weights file with the -w or --weights option:

java -jar phinder.jar -i documents/ -w my-weights.json

docs.title: Calculation
docs.text: The Magnitude Score is calculated as: Sum(PII Type Count * PII Type Weight)

docs.title: Density Score
docs.text:
Phinder also calculates a Density Score, which is the Magnitude Score divided by the word count of the document: Density Score = Magnitude Score / Word Count

This score helps identify documents with a high concentration of PII relative to their size.
